items.body:
The surreal comedian struggles to sell a middling and mostly conventional film about an uptight realtor whose life is upturned by an unpredictable figure from his past

items.hook: The Troubles: Bloody Friday: The Provisional IRA detonate 22 bombs in central Belfast, Northern Ireland, United Kingdom in the space of 80 minutes, killing nine and injuring 130.
items.body: The Troubles were an ethno-nationalist conflict in Northern Ireland that lasted for about 30 years from the late 1960s to 1998. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: HD 194783 is a solitary star located in the southern constellation of Microscopium near the border with Sagittarius.
items.body: It is barely visible to the naked eye as a bluish-white-hued point of light with an apparent magnitude of 6.08. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: At the conclusion of a fireworks display on Okura Beach in Akashi, Hyōgo, Japan, 11 people are killed and more than 120 are injured when a pedestrian footbridge connecting the beach to JR Asagiri Station becomes overcrowded and people leaving the event fall down in a domino effect.
items.body: Akashi is a city in southern Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Rum Rebellion of 1808 was a coup d'état in the British penal colony of New South Wales, staged by the New South Wales Corps in order to depose Governor William Bligh.
items.body: Australia's first and only military coup to date, its name derives from the illicit rum trade of early Sydney, over which the 'Rum Corps', as it became known, maintained a monopoly. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: First Indochina War: The Geneva Conference partitions Vietnam into North Vietnam and South Vietnam.
items.body: The First Indochina War, known alternatively internationally as the French Indochina War, was fought in French Indochina between France and the Viet Minh and their respective allies, from 19 December 1946 until 11 August 1954. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Apollo program: At 02:56 UTC, astronaut Neil Armstrong becomes the first person to walk on the Moon, followed 19 minutes later by Edwin "Buzz" Aldrin.
items.body: The Apollo program, also known as Project Apollo, was the United States human spaceflight program led by NASA, which landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Louis Rigolly, a Frenchman, becomes the first man to break the 100 mph (161 km/h) barrier on land. He drove a 15-liter Gobron-Brillié in Ostend, Belgium.
items.body: Louis Rigolly (1876–1958), a Frenchman, was the first man to drive a car at over 100 miles per hour (160 km/h). Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Farnoosh Moshiri is an Iranian-born novelist, playwright, and librettist.
items.body: She teaches creative writing and literature at University of Houston–Downtown. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Erden Eruç completes the first solo human-powered circumnavigation of the world.
items.body: Erden Eruç is a Turkish-American adventurer who became the first person in history to complete an entirely solo and entirely human-powered circumnavigation of the Earth on 21 July 2012 in Bodega Bay, California, United States. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Levée en masse is a French term used for a policy of mass national conscription, often in the face of invasion.
items.body: The concept originated during the French Revolutionary Wars, particularly for the period following 16 August 1793, when able-bodied men aged 18 to 25 were conscripted. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: St John the Baptist's Church is an Anglican church in the village of Mayfield, Staffordshire, England.
items.body: It is a Grade I listed building. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Godzilla v Kong actor Kaylee Hottle dies aged 18
items.body:
The deaf actor, who played a major role in Godzilla v Kong and its 2024 sequel, was killed in a car crash in Maryland

items.body:

items.hook: The Dink review – Jake Johnson’s pickleball comedy serves up engagingly silly laughs
items.body:

There’s some funny stuff here in this engagingly silly sports comedy from producer-star Jake Johnson about the world of pickl
